shortDescription: GCA, short for Go China Access, is a fixed-supply ERC-20 token on Base Mainnet. It is a concept-stage community direction focused on the Go China macro narrative, bilingual Web3 education, public market research, and planned access to non-custodial quant risk tools.
longDescription: GCA is a fixed-supply ERC-20 token deployed on Base Mainnet. GCA currently stands for Go China Access, a concept-stage community direction focused on the Go China macro narrative, bilingual Web3 education, public market research, and planned access to non-custodial quant risk tools. The intended Web3 Radar bridge is to make GCA an access and membership layer for liquidation replay reports, risk-warning credits, realistic backtests, ENTRY_READY signal review, position-size calculators, subscription discounts, and risk-control training. The contract created 1,000,000,000 GCA at deployment and includes no post-deployment minting, burning, taxes, blacklist, or admin controls. The official public pool is the Base Mainnet Uniswap v4 GCA/USDT pool, and the project has a disclosed owner-held reserve wallet. Third-party audit quote requests were submitted and then deferred by owner decision. The project is early-stage and should not be described as externally audited, price-backed, yield-bearing, or backed by guaranteed liquidity.

utilityPositioning.connectedProduct: Web3 Radar non-custodial quant risk toolkit
suitableUtility: one-time 100 Web3 Radar utility credits ledger record for each registered user who verifies one wallet holding at least 10000 GCA
GCA Member status in the Web3 Radar member ledger for each registered user who verifies one wallet holding at least 1000000 GCA

plannedFirstCampaign.walletLimit: one verified wallet per registered user
plannedFirstCampaign.creditExpiry: 180 days after ledger activation unless a later published policy extends it
plannedFirstCampaign.creditUse: reports, backtests, risk warnings, ENTRY_READY signal review, position calculators, or education access
plannedFirstCampaign.notCreditUse: cash, tokens, income, reimbursement, trading permission, or risk-control bypass

plannedMemberTier.registeredUserLimit: one membership status per registered user
plannedMemberTier.walletLimit: one verified wallet per registered user
plannedMemberTier.refreshCadence: 30 days after activation, or earlier if the user requests a manual recheck
plannedMemberTier.memberAccess: higher utility credit limits, member research notes, priority report queue, member training sessions, and priority support
plannedMemberTier.notMemberAccess: cash, tokens, income, reimbursement, voting control, guaranteed lifetime access, trading permission, or risk-control bypass

supportWorkflow.targetFirstResponse: 5-10 business days after controlled intake is live; not a guarantee

liquidity.liquidityDepthNote: Starter-depth only; trades can have high price impact and slippage.

marketQuality.nextAction: Use the market quality page before presenting market-growth updates; do not use artificial activity, self-trading, wash trading, or misleading volume.

supplyDisclosure.ownerReserveCustodyNote: Normal owner-controlled wallet; not locked, vested, or multisig-controlled.

listingReadiness.nextAction: Use this gate before CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, or centralized exchange outreach; current tracked listing submissions should wait for stronger legitimate liquidity depth, public activity, and exchange volume.

onchainProofs.nextAction: Use this public proof set for deployment, source verification, reserve transfer, official market, and functional swap evidence.

walletWarningEvidence.nextAction: Use this page if Blockaid, MetaMask, Uniswap, or another wallet-security reviewer asks for public evidence.

externalReviewStatus.nextAction: Use this public status page for BaseScan, Blockaid / MetaMask, GeckoTerminal, DEX Screener, CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, and audit review context.

reviewerKit.nextAction: Use this public reviewer kit when a platform wants one consolidated evidence packet.

platformReplies.nextAction: Use this public reply kit when a platform or moderator asks for a concise response template.

trustCenter.nextAction: Use this public verification hub when a platform, moderator, or wallet-security reviewer asks for the consolidated contract, market, supply, and review-status packet.

roadmap.nextAction: Use this public roadmap when reviewers ask what is live now, what is next, and what claims remain off-limits.

communityKit.nextAction: Use this public community kit before Telegram announcements or moderator replies.

dexScreener.nextAction: Use the pair URL; direct metadata is normally pulled from external token lists or Enhanced Token Info.

geckoTerminal.nextAction: Keep the official website linked to the GCA/USDT GeckoTerminal pool; do not submit duplicate requests unless project details change or GeckoTerminal asks for a correction.

coinGecko.nextAction: Wait for stronger public activity, social presence, and exchange volume before a listing request.

coinMarketCap.nextAction: Wait for stronger liquidity, material volume, and public activity before a tracked listing request.
